claim: Lex Fridman runs 3–4 hour interviews with frontier AI architects and his 2025 conversations with Amodei and Hassabis became 'landmark episodes that shaped public discourse'.
quote: Three-to-four-hour conversations with the architects of modern AI...pushed past talking points...landmark episodes that shaped public discourse

claim: Dwarkesh Patel is described as 'the primary oral historian of the artificial intelligence revolution' and is known for exhaustive preparation that challenges guests' premises.
quote: the primary oral historian of the artificial intelligence revolution...exhaustive preparation, technical depth, and willingness to challenge premises

claim: Dwarkesh has interviewed Karpathy, Sutskever, Zuckerberg, Musk, Nadella, Hassabis, and Dario Amodei; The Economist said he 'rose from nowhere to become Silicon Valley's favourite podcaster'.
quote: rose from nowhere to become Silicon Valley's favourite podcaster...interviewed prominent figures including AI researchers such as Andrej Karpathy and Ilya Sutskever, technology leaders such as Mark Zuckerberg, Elon Musk and Satya Nadella

claim: Latent Space is a top-10 US Tech podcast/newsletter aimed at AI Engineers and reached over 10 million readers and listeners in 2025.
quote: In 2025, over 10 million readers and listeners came to Latent Space to hear about news, papers and interviews in Software 3.0...top 10 in US Tech

claim: Machine Learning Street Talk is positioned as the highest-rated technical AI podcast on Spotify with an audience of engineers and research scientists, embracing 'the gnarly complexity of the tech'.
quote: highest-rated technical AI podcast on Spotify...embraces the gnarly complexity of the tech and conveys information at an extremely rare level of depth...not for the faint of heart

claim: Cognitive Revolution is hosted by Nathan Labenz (not 'Nathaniel Whittemore') with Erik Torenberg, biweekly, focused on builders and live-player analysis at the AI frontier.
quote: biweekly podcast where hosts Nathan Labenz and Erik Torenberg interview the builders on the edge of AI

claim: BG2Pod is a bi-weekly tech/markets podcast with Brad Gerstner and Bill Gurley, landing CEO-tier guests like Jensen Huang, Satya Nadella, and OpenAI's Nick Turley in 2025-2026.
quote: open source bi-weekly conversation with Brad Gerstner...& Bill Gurley...on all things tech, markets, investing & capitalism

claim: No Priors with Sarah Guo and Elad Gil is a weekly investor-perspective AI show whose 2026 forecast episode argues AI will 'stop being a reactive tool that waits for prompts' and become proactive.
quote: co-hosted by Elad Gil and Sarah Guo...biggest questions about AI including how far away AGI is, what markets are at risk for disruption

claim: Acquired (Ben Gilbert & David Rosenthal) is the #1 technology podcast on Apple/Spotify with 1M+ listeners per episode and monthly multi-hour deeply-researched company histories — best in class for tech-history.
quote: #1 technology podcast on Apple and Spotify, with each episode reaching over 1 million listeners...deeply researched monthly episodes about iconic businesses

claim: a16z runs a dedicated AI + a16z podcast focused on infrastructure, developer tooling, and the application layer, with regular Big Ideas yearly outlooks (e.g. Big Ideas 2026: The Agentic Interface).
quote: episodes on AI infrastructure, developer tooling, and the application layer

claim: Harry Stebbings runs The Twenty Minute VC (20VC), launched 20VC fund in 2020; the show's AI coverage in 2025 leans VC/markets and got mixed Apple Podcasts reviews ('opinionated people').
quote: Harry Stebbings is an English venture capitalist known for starting 'The Twenty Minute VC' podcast. In 2020, he launched his own venture capital fund called 20VC

claim: Patrick Collison does not host his own regular YouTube podcast but is a high-signal recurring guest (Dwarkesh, a16z, Cursor's Truell) — best treated as a guest signal rather than a channel.
quote: Patrick Collison (Stripe CEO) - Craft, Beauty, & The Future of Payments

claim: Industry roundups in 2026 consistently cluster Dwarkesh, Latent Space, MLST, No Priors, and Cognitive Revolution as the top-tier shows for AI engineers and founders, with Latent Space and MLST flagged as the most technical.
quote: Top Pods for AI Engineers and Founders
